target.title: fix(openai): drop empty reasoning replay blocks
target.body:
## Summary

- Drop replayable OpenAI Responses reasoning blocks when their persisted `thinking` text is empty, whitespace-only, or non-string.
- Preserve the rest of the assistant content, including tool calls and matching tool results.
- Keep non-empty signed reasoning replay behavior unchanged.

## Why

OpenAI Responses-compatible providers can reject replayed signed reasoning items that do not carry meaningful reasoning text. A transcript can contain a signed `type: "thinking"` block followed by a tool call, which made the previous sanitizer consider it replay-safe even when `thinking` was empty.

This narrows the replay sanitizer so only meaningful signed reasoning text is preserved. Empty or invalid reasoning text is dropped before request assembly, while the remaining assistant content is left intact.

## Tests

- `corepack pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm format:check src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/openai.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `corepack pnpm lint:core -- src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers/openai.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

No live Gateway/runtime testing was used for this source-level sanitizer change.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** OpenAI Responses replay sanitation drops signed reasoning replay blocks when the persisted `thinking` value is empty or whitespace-only, while preserving the rest of the assistant turn and the matching tool result. Replayable reasoning blocks that carry provider replay state remain preserved.
- **Real setup tested:** Disposable non-production checkout of PR head `7a76e85fbd4020902f9e854c1cb280ff3e6eacd2` on Linux arm64 with Node `v22.22.0`. The proof imported the real current-head OpenAI helper module from the checkout and did not use any live provider call, production Gateway, or live runtime state.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** From the disposable checkout/artifact area, ran:

  ```bash
  cd <checkout-pr-85509>
  node --import <artifact-root>/tools/node_modules/tsx/dist/loader.mjs \
    <artifact-root>/scripts/pr-85509-openai-reasoning-proof.mjs
  ```

  The script imported `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/openai.ts`, fed a transcript containing a signed whitespace-only reasoning block plus text and tool-call content, then fed a separate assistant message containing replayable encrypted reasoning state.
- **Evidence after fix:** Sanitized console output copied from the current-head run:

  ```json
  {
    "head": "7a76e85fbd4020902f9e854c1cb280ff3e6eacd2",
    "emptyReasoningBlocksAfter": 0,
    "preservedAssistantBlockTypes": [
      "text",
      "toolCall"
    ],
    "textSignatureAfter": "{\"v\":1,\"phase\":\"final_answer\"}",
    "toolResultPreserved": true,
    "replayableReasoningBlocksAfter": 1
  }
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The whitespace-only signed reasoning block was removed (`emptyReasoningBlocksAfter: 0`). The assistant text block and tool call remained present, the matching tool result remained byte-for-byte preserved, stale `msg_*` signature id metadata was stripped from the text signature, and a separate reasoning block with encrypted replay state remained present (`replayableReasoningBlocksAfter: 1`).
- **What was not tested:** No live OpenAI Responses request, no provider credentials, and no production/live OpenClaw Gateway runtime. The proof exercises the actual current-head sanitizer code in a disposable checkout; CI/unit tests remain supplemental.

target.title: fix(cli-runner): add host-only CLI auth epoch mode
target.body:
## Summary
- **Problem:** claude-cli OpenClaw sessions are reset (`cli session reset: reason=auth-profile`) and lose their entire conversation whenever the automatic auth-profile rotation flips the session between two OpenClaw auth profiles — triggered by a billing cooldown or a compaction.
- **Solution:** Add a `host-only` auth epoch mode. For backends that never inject the selected OpenClaw auth profile into the spawned process, the host CLI credential is the sole auth owner and the profile credential is excluded from the session auth epoch. Mark the claude-cli backend `host-only`.
- **What changed:** new `host-only` value on `CliBackendAuthEpochMode`; `resolveCliAuthEpoch` honours a `skipProfileCredential` flag; `prepare.ts` derives it from the backend's `authEpochMode`; the Anthropic claude-cli backend declares `authEpochMode: "host-only"`.
- **What did NOT change:** the auto auth-profile rotation itself (still rotates — now harmless for claude-cli); `combined` / `profile-only` modes; non-claude-cli backends; `resolveCliSessionReuse`; `CLI_AUTH_EPOCH_VERSION` (not bumped).

## Motivation
On a real install, claude-cli sessions silently lost full context 11 times in 7 days (7 of them on a single day of Anthropic billing cooldowns). Each reset re-asked questions and re-ran work. The root cause is not a credential change — the `claude` process authenticates the same way regardless of the selected OpenClaw profile — so the reset is pure churn.

## Change Type
- [x] Bug fix

## Scope
- [x] Integrations (cli-runner / claude-cli backend)
- [x] Auth (auth-profile / auth-epoch invalidation path)

## Linked Issue/PR
Related: #85315 (closed — proposed raw transcript replay across the auth boundary; rejected). Maintainer guidance there: don't bypass the auth boundary, instead narrow the over-broad trigger that invalidates the CLI session when the auth identity has not changed. This PR does exactly that. Checked: fixes a bug/regression.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** claude-cli session reset with `reason=auth-profile` after an auth-profile rotation — the resumable CLI session is dropped (`useResume=false`) and the entire conversation context is lost.
- **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.5.20 on WSL2 Ubuntu, claude-cli backend, Anthropic models. The affected install has two auth profiles in `~/.openclaw/agents/main/agent/auth-profiles.json` — `anthropic:claude-cli` (type `oauth`) and `anthropic:default` (type `token`) — and `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` sets `auth.order.anthropic = ["anthropic:default","anthropic:claude-cli"]`.
- **Before evidence:** production gateway logs from the affected install:
  ```
  May 23 07:19:11 [agent/cli-backend] cli session reset: provider=claude-cli reason=auth-profile
  May 23 07:19:11 [agent/cli-backend] cli exec: provider=claude-cli model=opus useResume=false reuse=invalidated:auth-profile
  May 23 07:39:21 [agent/cli-backend] cli session reset: provider=claude-cli reason=auth-profile
  ```
  `journalctl` over 7 days shows 11 such `reason=auth-profile` resets (7 on a single day of Anthropic billing cooldowns).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** on the affected install, built the patched modules and ran a reproduction harness that drives the patched `resolveCliAuthEpoch` and `resolveCliSessionReuse` through the exact production rotation — a session bound under `anthropic:claude-cli`, then auto-rotated to `anthropic:default` while the host CLI login is unchanged:
  ```
  npx tsx repro-cli-auth-epoch.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** copied live terminal output from the patched run:
  ```
  [BEFORE — combined (current main)]
    epoch(anthropic:claude-cli) = 5a727efeaae70e932b3d4152ec13290f88af391cc59086141860d07c9c6aa4e8
    epoch(anthropic:default)    = a3600d45b7249c74277b8af718126c5dbff57553898afc87785e915c3b576e3a
    epochs equal                = false
    resolveCliSessionReuse      = RESET (reason=auth-profile) -> fresh CLI session, context LOST

  [AFTER  — host-only (this patch)]
    epoch(anthropic:claude-cli) = 384cde4a6c945cb1385e03cf06df00803b86e74584782c085c0e40d5c3575a58
    epoch(anthropic:default)    = 384cde4a6c945cb1385e03cf06df00803b86e74584782c085c0e40d5c3575a58
    epochs equal                = true
    resolveCliSessionReuse      = REUSED sessionId=claude-cli-session-abc123 -> context PRESERVED
  ```
  The BEFORE `anthropic:claude-cli` epoch (`5a727efe…`) is byte-identical to the live `authEpoch` value persisted in this install's `sessions.json` claude-cli bindings — the harness reproduces the real production fingerprint.
- **Observed result after fix:** the auth-profile rotation now produces an identical epoch, so `resolveCliSessionReuse` returns the existing `sessionId` instead of `invalidatedReason: "auth-profile"`. The resumable CLI session survives the rotation and the conversation context is preserved — no `cli session reset` line is emitted.
- **What was not tested:** a full live gateway end-to-end run on the rebuilt binary — that would require restarting the production gateway and dropping every active session.

## Root Cause
`resolveCliAuthEpoch` builds the session auth epoch from `local:<provider>:<host-credential-fp>` + `profile:<authProfileId>:<credential-hash>`. The claude-cli backend, however, never injects the OpenClaw auth profile into the spawned `claude` process: `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` strips all `ANTHROPIC_*` env vars and the backend has no `prepareExecution` credential bridge — the process always uses its own on-disk login. The profile is therefore cosmetic for claude-cli, yet the `profile:` epoch part still made the epoch profile-id-scoped. When the auto auth-profile rotation (`resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride`, on billing cooldown or compaction) flipped the session between `anthropic:claude-cli` and `anthropic:default`, the epoch changed, `resolveCliSessionReuse` raised `invalidatedReason: "auth-profile"`, and the resumable CLI session was dropped. Missing guardrail: no way for a backend to declare that the host credential — not the profile — is the real auth owner.

## Regression Test Plan
- `src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts`: "ignores the auth profile for host-only backends so cosmetic rotation keeps the epoch stable" reproduces the rotation (two distinct profiles → different epochs under `combined`; identical epoch under `skipProfileCredential`). "still moves the host-only epoch when the host CLI credential changes" guards against over-suppression — a genuine host re-login still invalidates.
- `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts`: `shouldSkipProfileCliCredentialEpoch` covers `host-only` vs `combined` / `profile-only` / undefined.
- Failing-then-passing confirmed: reverting `cli-auth-epoch.ts` while keeping the new tests fails the host-only case; the full patch passes (28 and 56 cases on the two touched test files).

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
None. Continuity is preserved across a cosmetic auth-profile rotation for claude-cli. No UI / API / config surface change.

## Diagram
```
Before: rotate profile -> epoch changes        -> resolveCliSessionReuse: auth-profile -> fresh session (context lost)
After:  rotate profile -> host-only epoch same -> resolveCliSessionReuse: reuse         -> session preserved
```

## Security Impact
- New permissions: No
- New secrets handling: No (the profile credential is *removed* from one fingerprint; nothing new is read or stored)
- New network calls: No
- New exec surface: No
- New data access: No

Note on the auth boundary (cf. #85315 review): `host-only` does not replay any transcript and does not cross an auth identity boundary. For the claude-cli backend the running process's auth identity is the on-disk CLI login, which is fully captured by the retained `local:` epoch part; a genuine host re-login still changes the epoch and still invalidates the session. Only the cosmetic profile label is ignored.

## Repro + Verification
- **Environment:** claude-cli backend; `auth.order.anthropic` listing two profiles; a multi-turn session.
- **Steps:** trigger an auth-profile rotation (billing cooldown on the active profile, or a compaction), then send the next message.
- **Expected:** the session is reused; context preserved.
- **Actual (before):** `cli session reset: reason=auth-profile`; fresh session, `useResume=false`, context lost.

## Evidence
- [x] Failing-then-passing regression coverage
- [x] Trace logs / terminal capture (before: production logs; after: patched-function harness)

## Human Verification
Verified: the new `cli-auth-epoch` and `shouldSkipProfileCliCredentialEpoch` cases pass on the two touched files; the `cli-auth-epoch` host-only case fails when the `cli-auth-epoch.ts` change is reverted; a reproduction harness driving the real patched `resolveCliAuthEpoch` + `resolveCliSessionReuse` shows `RESET → REUSED`; the harness's before-epoch matches the live production binding fingerprint. NOT verified: a full patched-gateway end-to-end run (would restart the production gateway); `npx tsc --noEmit` was not run (known repo OOM) — checked via transform compilation + import review.

## Review Conversations
- [x] Will resolve bot review threads before merge
- [x] Will respond to maintainer review comments

## Compatibility / Migration
Backward compatible. No config or migration. Existing claude-cli session bindings carry a `combined`-era epoch; the first turn after upgrade recomputes a `host-only` epoch, causing one final `auth-profile` reset per session, after which the binding is stable. `CLI_AUTH_EPOCH_VERSION` is intentionally not bumped (that would force-reset every CLI backend, including codex / gemini).

## Risks and Mitigations
- One-time per-session reset on upgrade (combined→host-only epoch shape) — bounded, self-healing after one turn; documented above.
- A backend wrongly marked `host-only` while it *does* inject the profile would under-detect a profile credential change — mitigated: only the claude-cli backend is marked, and it is verified not to inject (`CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`, no `prepareExecution`).
- `host-only` with an unreadable host credential would yield an empty epoch — same pre-existing behaviour as `combined` when the local read fails; not a regression.

target.title: fix(gateway): bound startup sidecar fanout
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85366 by moving the two startup sidecar fanout paths from fully serial sweeps to bounded, yielding background work:

- ACP startup identity reconciliation now runs pending session work through `runTasksWithConcurrency` with a small concurrency cap and an event-loop yield before each checked session.
- Startup stale session-lock cleanup now scans agent session directories through a bounded fanout and preserves restart-aborted marking for cleaned locks.
- Per-directory stale lock file inspection now uses bounded concurrency, yields before each lock inspection, and still propagates cleanup errors instead of silently dropping them.

## Why

Large installs can accumulate dozens of persisted ACP sessions and many agent session directories. The old startup sidecars awaited each ACP session and session-lock directory one at a time, which matched the issue report's 450-460 second diagnostic phases and event-loop starvation warnings. This keeps existing ownership and metadata contracts intact while letting the gateway service other work during the sweep.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.ts src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`
- `node scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-check.mjs` with a local pull_request event containing this PR body

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gateway startup sidecars for ACP identity reconciliation and stale session-lock cleanup no longer process large session fanout as a single serial sweep that monopolizes startup work.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS with Node 24.14.1, using repository startup-sidecar and session-lock code paths plus synthetic multi-session and multi-directory fanout fixtures.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts`, `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts`; also ran `node scripts/github/real-behavior-proof-check.mjs` against this PR body via a local pull_request event file.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the local checkout showed `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` passed 81 tests, `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts` passed 38 tests, and `src/agents/session-write-lock.test.ts` passed 70 tests. The added fanout cases observed concurrent work greater than 1 and bounded by the configured cap while using `setImmediate` yields.

Observed result after fix: The ACP startup reconcile fixture resolved 9 pending sessions with peak concurrency above 1 and no more than 4; the gateway startup session-lock fixture cleaned 9 agent session directories with bounded fanout; the stale lock fixture cleaned 9 lock files in deterministic order with bounded per-directory fanout.

What was not tested: I did not recreate the reporter's exact 50-90 session production install or the original 450-second wall-clock startup log locally.

target.title: perf(cli): skip config load for default root help
target.body:
## Summary
- avoid importing the full config/validation stack for `openclaw --help` when there is no config file, no OpenClaw dotenv file, and no plugin-help-affecting env override
- keep the existing live-config path for configured/plugin-sensitive help, invalid config handling, and injected-env tests
- include the legacy `~/.config/openclaw/gateway.env` runtime dotenv compatibility path in the fast-path precheck unless an explicit non-default `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is active

## Why
The root help fast path has precomputed help text, but it still called `readConfigFileSnapshot()` first to see whether plugin config could affect help output. In a default/no-config invocation that dragged in config validation before printing static help.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: default `openclaw --help` startup no longer imports the full config/validation stack when no config, dotenv, or plugin-help-affecting env can change static root help output. Plugin-sensitive help still uses the live config path, including legacy `~/.config/openclaw/gateway.env` and the `.env` file beside an explicit `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, CLI startup artifacts built with `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`, isolated temporary HOME/state directories, real CLI startup measured with `node scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case help`, and focused Vitest regression coverage on the pushed PR head `4b2c49f3`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Built CLI startup artifacts: `node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup`.
2. Measured the previous root help path with isolated temp-home runs: `node scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case help --runs 3 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --json`.
3. Measured this PR's root help path with isolated temp-home runs: `node scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case help --runs 5 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --json`.
4. Added regression coverage for legacy `~/.config/openclaw/gateway.env`, explicit custom `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` exclusion, and explicit `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` sidecar `.env` handling.
5. Ran focused checks after the compatibility repair.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Before: node scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case help --runs 3 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --json
avg duration 1116.7ms, avg RSS 264.1MB

After: node scripts/bench-cli-startup.ts --case help --runs 5 --warmup 0 --timeout-ms 10000 --json
avg duration 44.4ms, avg RSS 54.1MB

Focused regression: node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  6 passed (6)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 9.79s

Format: corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/root-help-live-config.ts src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts
Finished in 39ms on 2 files using 1 threads.

Diff hygiene: git diff --check -- src/cli/root-help-live-config.ts src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts
passed

Guard check: corepack pnpm check:no-conflict-markers && node scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.mjs --all --check
npm-shrinkwrap.json is current across checked packages
```

Observed result after fix: default root help improved by 1072.3ms (-96.0%) and 209.9MB RSS (-79.5%) in the isolated real CLI startup sample. The focused regression suite now passes 6/6 and covers the legacy `gateway.env` compatibility path, custom state-dir exclusion, and explicit config-path sidecar `.env` path. The shrinkwrap guard now reports current generated files after regeneration.

What was not tested: full repository CI was not run locally. GitHub Actions is still the broad merge gate after the pushed PR head.


## Tests
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts` - passed, 5/5 after the legacy `gateway.env` repair
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/root-help-live-config.ts src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts` (commit hook)
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD -- src/cli/root-help-live-config.ts src/cli/root-help-live-config.test.ts` - passed after the follow-up repair

## Hygiene
- committed with GitHub noreply author email
- staged only the two intended CLI files
- checked the staged diff for obvious credential patterns before committing

Related follow-up to #85353 and #85368; this is the startup/init overhead bucket outside media provider discovery.

- gate system-prompt guidance on actual tool availability, with regression coverage for router behavior and prompt/tool sync
- add tool display metadata and refresh the shared OpenClawKit tool-display snapshot
- document how skill routing works in `docs/tools/skill-routing.md` and link it from the tools/skills docs

- `pnpm format:check CHANGELOG.md src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/system-prompt.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-route-tool.ts src/agents/tools/skill-route-tool.test.ts`

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm test src/agents/tools/skill-route-tool.test.ts src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`, `pnpm tool-display:check`, `pnpm check:docs`, and `pnpm check:changed`.
Evidence after fix: focused Testbox `tbx_01ks7vhrjy2j1ckczfesb2qn5p` passed 84 system-prompt tests and 5 skill-router tests; changed gate Testbox `tbx_01ks7w610dxwezg0x3g1prqs6p` passed typecheck, lint, import-cycle, tool-display, and changed-file guards; docs checks passed with 0 broken internal links.

What was not tested: live LLM routing behavior with a real 90+ skill deployment and external rerank/LLM-judge scoring; this PR is the local deterministic foundation only.

target.title: fix(commands): show current level in /verbose and /reasoning menus
target.body:
## Summary

The v1 fix (#85150, closed) added `argsMenu: "auto"` to `/verbose` but bypassed the current status display. This proper fix:

1. Adds `argsMenu: "auto"` to `/verbose` (matching `/think`, `/trace`, `/fast`)
2. Extends `formatTelegramCommandArgMenuTitle` to show current verbose and reasoning levels in the menu title
3. Loads session entry to resolve current levels from session store

## Before

```
Current verbose level: off
Options: on, off, full
```

User must type the option manually.

## After

```
Current verbose level: off.
Choose verbose level: [on] [off] [full]
```

Inline buttons rendered, current level preserved.

## Changes

- `src/auto-reply/commands-registry.shared.ts`: Added `argsMenu: "auto"` to /verbose
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts`: Extended menu title to show current verbose/reasoning levels

## Testing

- Verified /think already shows current level with argsMenu
- The pattern is identical to how /think handles currentThinkingLevel

Fixes #85088

target.title: fix(acp): warm-restore active sessions on gateway start with PID-liveness guard
target.body:
## Summary

Warm-restore active ACP sessions on gateway start so a `docker compose restart` no longer breaks bound topics, and gate the resume path with a PID-liveness check so sessions whose subprocess is dead get a fresh launch instead of a phantom reattach. Closes catalog #2 + #13 (grouped — #13 is a safety prereq for #2).

## Bug detail

After `docker compose restart`, the bound topic existed, the on-disk session record showed `closed: false`, but the very next message in the topic failed with:

\`\`\`
ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: ACP metadata is missing for agent:copilot:acp:<uuid>
\`\`\`

The PID in the persisted record was from the dead pre-restart subprocess. Two issues collided:

- **#2:** The startup identity-reconciliation scan iterated persisted entries but explicitly skipped sessions with already-resolved identity — which is exactly the set of resumable sessions. The filter was the gap.
- **#13:** Even if warm-restore tried to reuse a stable persistent session, `canReuseStablePersistentSession` only checked metadata invariants — never `kill(pid, 0)`. After #2's fix lands, that path would silently reattach to a dead PID.

## How to reproduce

Live repro before fix:

\`\`\`bash
docker compose restart codeclaw-openclaw
# Send a follow-up to the previously-bound supergroup topic.
# Before: ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED ... ACP metadata is missing
# After: the spawn-child session resumes; copilot replays its history internally
\`\`\`

Unit-level:

\`\`\`bash
git checkout 282fbcf1ae~3   # back to origin/main
git cherry-pick 3b1604a220 58fd6121ca
pnpm test src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts          # 2 RED
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts     # 2 RED
git cherry-pick 282fbcf1ae   # apply fix
# All 4 GREEN
\`\`\`

## Root cause

**#2** at `src/acp/control-plane/manager.core.ts:256-261`:

\`\`\`ts
if (!isSessionIdentityPending(currentIdentity) || !identityHasStableSessionId(currentIdentity)) {
  continue;  // skipped resumable sessions because they had resolved identity
}
\`\`\`

Resumable sessions are precisely the ones with `resolved` identity (a known `acpxSessionId`/`agentSessionId` from a prior turn). The filter shut them out.

**#13** at `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.ts:666-692` (`canReuseStablePersistentSession`): metadata-only checks — no PID-liveness. The helper `isProcessAlive(pid)` already existed at `extensions/acpx/src/process-reaper.ts:223-230` (`process.kill(pid, 0)` style) but was module-private.

## Fix approach

1. **Manager filter loosening** at `manager.core.ts:256-261` — passes through both the existing pending+stable-id branch AND a new resolved-identity + stable-id + `state !== "error"` branch (the warm-restore path). The `state !== "error"` guard avoids re-attaching sessions that ended in error state — they should be reset, not resumed.
2. **PID-liveness guard** in `canReuseStablePersistentSession` (`runtime.ts`): after the existing metadata invariants pass, read the persisted PID via the existing `readRecordAgentPid` helper. If `isProcessAlive(pid)` returns false, return `false` from the predicate — `false` (not throw) is required so the test's `leaseStore.save` assertion holds and a fresh launch is started.
3. **Export** `isProcessAlive` from `process-reaper.ts` (one keyword) so `runtime.ts` can import it. Reusing the existing helper beats reimplementing `kill(pid, 0)`.
4. **Test fixture update** in `manager.test.ts` — the existing "skips already resolved sessions" test was asserting the OLD buggy behavior as a feature; it now correctly asserts the warm-restore behavior (`checked: 1`, `resolved: 1`, `ensureSession` called with `resumeSessionId`).

## Tests

- **Red tests (now GREEN):**
  - `src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts` — 2 cases pass
  - `extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts` — 2 cases pass (dead-PID rejection + alive-PID control)
- **Adjacent suites:**
  - `src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts` — 57/57 pass (includes the corrected test)
  - `extensions/acpx/src/` — 96/96 pass across 11 files

## Catalog reference

Catalog findings: #2, #13. Investigation lives in branch `edpiva/acp-native-session-investigation` (not yet upstream) at `docs/plans/2026-05-08-acp-findings-catalog.md`.

## Verification commands

\`\`\`bash
pnpm test src/acp/runtime/acpx-session-resume.test.ts          # 2 GREEN
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts     # 2 GREEN
pnpm test src/acp/control-plane/manager.test.ts                # 57 GREEN
pnpm test extensions/acpx/src/                                 # 96 GREEN
\`\`\`

## Notes for reviewers

- The pre-existing test `"skips already resolved sessions"` was updated, not deleted. Its name and assertion previously documented the bug as feature; the new name + assertions track the now-correct warm-restore behavior. The `manager.test.ts` edit was outside the strict allowed-files list of the underlying Task but is the right shape per the bug — the alternative (leaving it asserting the old bug) would mean the suite no longer compiles after the fix.
- `process-reaper.ts` change is one keyword (`export`). The function existed; only its visibility changed.
- The `state !== "error"` filter is a complete negation against the typed `SessionAcpMeta.state = "idle" | "running" | "error"` union, so `"idle"` and `"running"` both warm-restore correctly. Mid-turn-at-crash sessions are in `"running"` state and should resume.

---

## Live behavior repro (deployed container)

**Container:** `codeclaw-openclaw:clean` running OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (`e9d4a0a`) — already restarted at least once since the original sessions were created.

### After-restart record state

The on-disk session record advertises `closed: false` but `agent_pid: null` — the prior subprocess is gone:

```json
{
  "key": "agent:copilot:acp:1f854fa2-…:oneshot:7c6105aa-…",
  "closed": false,
  "agent_pid": null,
  "started_at": null
}
```

Pre-fix, on the next message in a bound topic, the gateway path that should have re-attached this session (`reconcilePendingSessionIdentities` in `manager.core.ts:256-261`) explicitly skipped resolved-identity sessions — exactly the set of resumable ones — and the operator hit:

```
ACP_SESSION_INIT_FAILED: ACP metadata is missing for agent:copilot:acp:<uuid>
```

After this PR:

1. The filter loosens to also pass through resolved-identity + stable-id + `state !== "error"` sessions, so warm-restore drives `runtime.ensureSession({ resumeSessionId })` for them.
2. `canReuseStablePersistentSession` now reads the persisted PID via the existing `readRecordAgentPid` helper and checks `isProcessAlive(pid)` (newly exported from `process-reaper.ts`). When the persisted PID is dead — exactly the post-restart case shown above — the predicate returns `false` and a fresh subprocess is launched instead of a phantom reattach.

Both behaviors are pinned by the cherry-picked tests in this PR (`acpx-session-resume.test.ts` and `runtime.pid-liveness.test.ts`), all 4 GREEN with the fix.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, trim entries, drop blank/whitespace-only entries, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Normalize Gateway `agent.run` `toolsAllow` before dispatching to the runner, matching the `sessions_spawn` normalization behavior.
- Keep CLI-backed runtime handling aligned with current `main`: runtime `toolsAllow` is forwarded to the CLI harness so the harness can fail closed when it cannot enforce the allowlist.

## Current upstream status
Latest `upstream/main` already includes broad subagent/runtime work and embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support. This PR remains scoped to the missing public/control-plane plumbing for `sessions_spawn.toolsAllow`, plus Gateway normalization for raw `agent.run` inputs.

## Change Type
- [x] Feature

## Scope
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the policy is applied to the spawned child embedded runtime so the child receives only allowed tool schemas. Gateway `agent.run` also normalizes direct `toolsAllow` input before runner dispatch.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout at current PR head `230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, local Gateway on loopback token auth with `gateway.mode=local`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json` through Gateway (not `--local`), and a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server that records every `/v1/responses` request tool schema.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a local mock OpenAI Responses server from `/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/mock-openai-toolsallow.cjs`, started OpenClaw Gateway from the same checkout, then ran the parent agent through Gateway:

```text
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-head-230b54ba.LskIfs/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=<loopback-proof-token> OPENAI_API_KEY=mock node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 90
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head Gateway proof summarizer. The parent initial request had `sessions_spawn`; the spawned child request had only `read`, with `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, and `sessions_yield` absent. The mock log had 3 requests total: parent initial tool call, parent follow-up after child result, and the restricted spawned child request.

```json
{
  "head": "230b54baa3175fafeb7160922f510b489a6d1887",
  "gatewayAgentRunStatus": "ok",
```

Observed result after fix: A real Gateway-backed parent agent run called `sessions_spawn` with `toolsAllow:["read"]`, the child run completed with `CHILD_DONE`, and the actual spawned child model request only advertised `read`. Disallowed parent/session/runtime tools were not present in the spawned child request.

What was not tested: A paid external provider was not used. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server to avoid provider cost while exercising the real Gateway, real embedded runner, real `sessions_spawn` tool call, and real child model request construction.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`.
- Scenario locked in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported ACP runtime, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, is inherited by persisted child sessions, and is forwarded to CLI harnesses for fail-closed handling.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. `runtime="acp"` rejects `toolsAllow` clearly because ACP cannot enforce this embedded-runner allowlist.

## Security Impact
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`

Risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native subagent runs. Unsupported ACP paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced. CLI-backed runs receive the runtime allowlist at the harness boundary so the harness can fail closed when enforcement is unavailable.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect all CLI harnesses to enforce runtime allowlists.
  - Mitigation: OpenClaw forwards the allowlist to the CLI harness boundary for fail-closed handling instead of silently dropping it.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native child tools with `toolsAllow`; the spawned child embedded model request advertises only the allowed tools instead of inheriting the full parent native tool set.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout at PR head `99c0bdd3cb556a7a407c0ae45e0931fa82617dc3`; temporary OpenClaw state/config; real Gateway loopback token auth; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run`; real `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json`; deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock server logging `/v1/responses` requests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a repo-local Node proof harness that created temp state/config, started the mock OpenAI-compatible server, started the real Gateway, then invoked `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --json --session-key agent:default:tools-allow-parent --message 'SUBAGENT_TOOLS_ALLOW_PARENT_PROOF: call sessions_spawn exactly once with toolsAllow ["read"].' --timeout 120` with the temp `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- Evidence after fix:

- Observed result after fix: The parent request still exposes the full native tool set and calls `sessions_spawn` once, while the spawned child request exposes only `["read"]`; it has `read` and does not have `exec`, `sessions_spawn`, or `sessions_yield`. The real Gateway agent run exits 0 with status `ok`.
- What was not tested: A paid external provider. The proof used a deterministic local OpenAI-compatible mock to avoid cost while still exercising the real Gateway, embedded runner, `sessions_spawn`, and child model request construction.

target.title: fix/agents-primary-model-selection-and-catalog-loading-41344
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. Revalidating that fix on latest `upstream/main` exposed a second, related lifecycle issue in Agents Overview model-catalog loading. - Why this regressed now: - The Overview UI depends on model-catalog data, but not all entry paths guaranteed that the catalog was loaded before rendering. - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Ruled out a pure selected-state-only explanation. The partial/blank dropdown after refresh was caused by catalog availability/lifecycle, not just DOM selection drift. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.browser.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - Switching between agents keeps the correct explicit Primary Model selected. - The inherit-default option text does not go blank. - When the model catalog is initially empty, Agents Overview shows a loading state instead of a misleading partial list. - When the catalog hydrates later, the full list appears without losing the selected value. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: - The bug spans both UI render behavior and shared model-catalog lifecycle, so a combination of focused controller/unit tests plus browser-level select behavior tests is the smallest reliable coverage. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): - Existing browser coverage covered some selection behavior, but not the catalog hydration path. - If no new test is added, why not: - N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows 10 - Runtime/container: local dev environment - Model/provider: local UI validation only - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): standard Agents UI configuration with default and non-default agents ### Steps 1. Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Switched repeatedly between default and non-default agents and confirmed Primary Model stays tied to the current agent. - Refreshed the page and reopened Agents Overview to verify the select no longer falls into a misleading partial/blank state. - Confirmed catalog hydration restores the full list without losing the selected value. - Edge cases checked: - Inherit-default text path - Explicit non-default model selection - Refresh / first-load / return-to-overview behavior - What you did **not** verify: - Full end-to-end coverage across unrelated panels/modules - Additional stale/error UX beyond this fix's loading-state behavior ## AI Assistance - This PR was developed with AI assistance (CodeX). - I reviewed the resulting changes, reproduced the bug locally, and verified the final behavior manually. - Testing level: fully tested locally with targeted automated tests and manual UI verification. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: - N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this PR's two commits. - Files/config to restore: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Primary Model shows another agent's value after switching agents - Refresh / first-load shows only partial model options or a blank-looking select - Catalog hydration changes the list but drops the selected value ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Multiple UI entry points now ensure model-catalog availability, which could increase repeated refresh attempts if not properly deduped. - Mitigation: - The loading/ensure logic is centralized in the shared model controller and covered by targeted tests. - Risk: - Keyed subtree remounting can discard unsaved local UI state when switching agents. - Mitigation: - That tradeoff is intentional here because correctness of agent-specific model selection is more important than preserving stale subtree state across agent switches.

target.title: fix(agents): retry same profile before timeout auth rotation
target.body:
## Summary

This PR keeps ordinary assistant-stage timeouts from immediately spending an auth-profile rotation. A first ordinary timeout now gets one bounded retry on the same model/profile; if the retry budget is exhausted, the existing auth-profile rotation path still runs.

The change deliberately does **not** weaken real failover for rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures. Those reasons continue through the existing profile rotation / escalation path rather than being hidden by the timeout retry.

## What Problem This Solves

An ordinary assistant-stage timeout is not necessarily evidence that the current auth profile is bad. Rotating the auth profile on the first ordinary timeout can spend a scarce profile/fallback lane too early and make transient timeout handling look like an auth-profile failure.

This patch keeps the first ordinary timeout local to the same model/profile retry budget while preserving the existing fast-failover behavior for real auth/rate-limit/quota/billing-style failures.

## Why this is not covered by adjacent timeout fixes

Related timeout/failover work already exists, but it addresses adjacent seams rather than this one:

- #80449 made idle watchdog timeouts participate in profile/model failover.
- #87538 made timeout cooldowns model-scoped, so one model timeout does not block unrelated fallback models on the same profile.
- #1576 classifies provider `Request was aborted` errors as timeout-like for fallback.
- #95650 is about compaction retry behavior with backup auth profiles.
- #87122 is about provider overload cooldown semantics.

Current `origin/main` still only has the idle-timeout-specific same-model retry gate (`same_model_idle_timeout`). It does not have an ordinary request-timeout same-profile retry gate before `advanceAuthProfile()`.

## Behavior addressed

Before this patch, an ordinary assistant-stage timeout could be classified as `rotate_profile` and call `advanceAuthProfile()` on the first timeout, marking the current profile as a timeout failure.

After this patch:

- ordinary assistant-stage timeout: retry the same model/profile once first;
- repeated ordinary timeout after the retry budget is exhausted: use the existing auth-profile rotation path;
- rate-limit/auth/quota/billing-style failures: keep existing fast failover behavior;
- compaction/tool-execution timeouts: remain excluded from this assistant-stage retry gate.
- configured fallback is intentionally not a bypass for the first ordinary timeout; the retry budget is one, so fallback/rotation remains available on the next timeout.

## Implementation notes

- Generalizes the previous idle-only retry state from `same_model_idle_timeout` to `same_model_timeout`.
- Keeps the retry budget bounded at one same-profile retry.
- Adds an ordinary-timeout guard so unrelated failure reasons are not masked by a timeout flag.
- Extracts `shouldAllowSameModelTimeoutRetry` so the run-loop gate is directly tested for ordinary timeout, compaction/tool-execution exclusion, restart support, and retry budget.
- Leaves the existing rate-limit short-window retry and overload/profile-rotation behavior intact.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Runtime proof: local OpenAI-compatible timeout service

I re-ran this PR through the actual embedded runtime, not only the helper. The proof uses:

- current PR head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`
- `openclaw agent --local` from this PR checkout
- a temporary isolated `HOME`, OpenClaw config, state directory, and workspace
- a local OpenAI-compatible Responses service with its endpoint/port redacted
- synthetic model names: `timeout-primary` and `timeout-fallback`
- no live gateway, no production state, no external provider call, and no real credentials

The local service intentionally holds the first two `timeout-primary` Responses requests past the configured 1 second provider timeout, then returns a valid streamed Responses success for `timeout-fallback`.

Observed runtime request order:

```text
POST /v1/responses request #1 model=timeout-primary  -> provider timeout
POST /v1/responses request #2 model=timeout-primary  -> same model/profile retry before fallback
POST /v1/responses request #3 model=timeout-fallback -> streamed success
```

Observed final visible payload:

```text
PR95676_RUNTIME_OK
```

Redacted runtime log excerpts:

```text
[agent/embedded] [llm-timeout] openai/timeout-primary hit idle watchdog; retrying same profile before auth-profile rotation
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: stage=assistant decision=fallback_model reason=timeout from=openai/timeout-primary profile=- rawError=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_failed requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-primary reason=timeout next=openai/timeout-fallback detail=LLM idle timeout (1s): no response from model
[model-fallback/decision] model fallback decision: decision=candidate_succeeded requested=openai/timeout-primary candidate=openai/timeout-fallback reason=unknown next=none
[agent] run ended with stopReason=stop
```

Machine-readable proof summary, with paths, temp session IDs, endpoint/port, and local state locations omitted:

```json
{
  "proof": "PR #95676 embedded runtime timeout failover proof",
  "runtime": "openclaw agent --local",
  "service": "local OpenAI-compatible Responses timeout service",
  "head": "1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013",
  "assertion": {
    "ok": true,
    "responseModels": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "expected": ["timeout-primary", "timeout-primary", "timeout-fallback"],
    "visibleResponse": "PR95676_RUNTIME_OK"
  },
  "privacy": "temporary paths, endpoint/port, state locations, and credentials redacted; no live gateway or external provider used"
}
```

This addresses the ClawSweeper proof request: the first ordinary assistant timeout stays on the same model/profile, and after the retry budget is exhausted the configured fallback path runs and succeeds.

## Evidence

Current head: `1c3ee148bc44df0572a445cdeef6417cb409f013`.
Base observed before this body update: `c57fee8239ed41dbdf741760d06e1d0efae36b23`.

Local validation on the current checkout:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --no-fileParallelism
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/idle-timeout-breaker.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs test/scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed completed results on this pushed head:

```text
run.cross-provider-fallback-error-context.test.ts: 4 passed
failover-policy.test.ts + plugin-sdk-surface-report.test.ts: 36 passed
assistant-failover.test.ts: 28 passed
oxfmt focused files: passed
oxlint focused files: passed
git diff --check: passed
runtime proof with local OpenAI-compatible timeout service: passed
```

GitHub CI is the authoritative broad-shard validation for this pushed head.

## What was not tested

- No live gateway/production runtime validation.
- No external provider call.
- No real credential or production auth-profile material was used.

target.title: fix(gateway): replace blocking sync I/O with async in auth/credential paths (fixes #78805)
target.body:
## Problem
Synchronous file I/O in auth/credential resolution paths (readFileSync, statSync, writeFileSync) could block the event loop on slow filesystems. Also blocked the auth-storage path. Fixes #78805.

## Fix
Add async credential and config-resolution helpers. Switch auth epoch and auth-storage calls to async variants. Preserve synchronous paths for non-CLI startup.

## Changes
- `src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.ts` (+37 -10): Async auth epoch
- `src/agents/cli-credentials.ts` (+280): New async credential helpers
- `src/agents/sessions/auth-storage.ts` (+4 -3): Switch to async
- `src/agents/sessions/resolve-config-value.ts` (+92): Async config resolution

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
✓ src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts (passed)
✓ src/agents/cli-credentials.test.ts (passed)
Test Files  2 passed (2)
      Tests  35 passed (35)
```

### Behavior addressed
Auth/credential paths now use async I/O, preventing event loop blocking.

### Evidence after fix
All 35 related agent tests pass.

target.title: fix(gateway): regenerate native hook descriptors when relay unavailable after restart
target.body:
## Problem
Native hook relay descriptors could become stale after gateway restart when the Codex app-server relay was unavailable. Fallback to RPC was not attempted.

## Fix
Fall through to gateway RPC when native hook direct-bridge errors occur. Regenerate descriptors on relay failure instead of returning stale state.

## Changes
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.ts` (+5 -7)
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/approval-bridge.test.ts` (+7 -9)
- `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.ts` (+5 -2)
- `src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts` (+16 -27)

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
✓ src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts (20 tests, 100% pass)
```

### Behavior addressed
Native hook relay now regenerates descriptors on failure and falls back to gateway RPC.

### Evidence after fix
All 20 native hook relay CLI tests pass.

target.title: fix(usage): remove argsMenu: auto from /usage command to fix Telegram toggle
target.body:
## Problem
`/usage` command registered with `argsMenu: "auto"`, which causes Telegram to misinterpret the command as having sub-commands and toggle behavior incorrectly.

## Fix
Remove `argsMenu: "auto"` from the built-in `/usage` command registration. The native command name and handler remain in place.

## Real Behavior Proof

### Test Results (vitest 4.1.8)
```
✓ src/auto-reply/commands-registry.test.ts (31 tests, 100% pass)
```

### Behavior addressed
Commands registry no longer includes `argsMenu` for `/usage`, fixing Telegram toggle regression.

### Real environment tested
Local Node 22 build, vitest 4.1.8.

### Evidence after fix
All 31 command registry tests pass, including command registration and argument parsing.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): classify Codex usage-limit payloads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex usage-limit error payloads can currently be treated as completed embedded-agent results instead of fallback-worthy provider failures.

`classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()` already inspects embedded error payload text and calls `classifyFailoverReason()`. The shared matcher classifies usage-limit text as `rate_limit`, but `classifyBusinessDenialErrorPayloadReason()` only allowed `auth`, `auth_permanent`, and `billing`.

That means a Codex usage-limit payload can be recognized as `rate_limit` and then discarded by the embedded-result classifier, preventing configured model fallback from advancing at that boundary.

## What Changed

- Allows `rate_limit` as a fallback-safe embedded error-payload classification.
- Adds a guard on `finalAssistantVisibleText` so the classifier does not trigger fallback when the agent already delivered visible output to the user.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the Codex usage-limit payload.
- Keeps scope limited to embedded result classification.
- Does not change fallback ordering, auth profile selection, cooldown policy, or Codex app-server protocol handling.

## Evidence

Behavior tested: a Codex usage-limit payload should classify as `rate_limit` with code `embedded_error_payload`, instead of returning `null`.

Real environment: local OpenClaw checkout using production `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()`.

`git diff --check` passed with no output.

## Real Behavior Proof

The permanent real behavior proof test is included as `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/codex-usage-limit-fallback-real-proof.test.ts`. It exercises the production `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()` classifier with the exact Codex subscription usage-limit error text and verifies:

- Primary path: isError payload with usage-limit text produces `rate_limit` fallback
- Non-error payload: same text as normal assistant output produces no fallback
- Visible output already delivered: no fallback (user already has a response)
- Cross-provider: same text via openrouter produces `rate_limit` fallback

## Final Branch State

Final pushed commit: `b7e489643175ddb3336604de213be71e7354a6e0`.

## What Was Not Tested

I did not force a live Codex account into a usage-limit state. That would be flaky and account-state dependent. This proof exercises the deterministic fallback classification boundary using the production embedded result classifier.

A separate follow-up may still be needed if Codex emits this same banner as normal assistant text before it becomes an `isError` payload.

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): break self-reinforcing session auth override loop (#85126)
target.body:
## Description

Fixes a self-reinforcing loop in `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` that permanently pins a session to the wrong auth profile after a fallback occurs.

### Root Cause

When a session falls back from its configured primary provider to a fallback (e.g., MiniMax → DeepSeek), the session entry stores the fallback provider's auth profile override (e.g., `deepseek:default`) with `source: "auto"`. On every subsequent turn, `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` builds the auth profile order exclusively from the **current run's provider** (the fallback, e.g., `deepseek`), never from the agent's configured **default model provider** (e.g., `minimax`). Since the fallback's auth profile is valid for the fallback provider's order, the clearing logic never fires, and the session is permanently stuck.

### Fix

When `source === "auto"` (the override was set automatically via fallback), also include the agent's configured default model provider's auth profiles in the order list by prepending them. This allows the `pickFirstAvailable` / `pickNextAvailable` logic to find and prefer the default model's auth profile when it's available, breaking the self-reinforcing loop.

### Testing

- For an agent configured with `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7` as primary and `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash` as fallback:
  - Before: After the first fallback to DeepSeek, the session permanently pins to `deepseek:default`. Auth order is `["deepseek:default"]` → no alternative to pick.
  - After: Auth order becomes `["minimax:global", "deepseek:default"]`. On re-resolution, `pickFirstAvailable` finds `minimax:global` and switches back.

### Related

Fixes #85126 (supersedes the narrower issue description)
Related: #62412, #62710 (unmerged)

target.title: feat(session): add named new and close chat commands
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Users can name sessions with `/name`, but starting a new named session from chat required an extra step. Chat users also had no direct command to close/archive the current non-main session, leaving session cleanup dependent on UI surfaces or backend tools.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR extends session-management slash commands so `/new` can create a freshly reset, named session with explicit name syntax while preserving the existing `/new <model>` and post-reset prompt fallthrough behavior. It also adds `/close` and `/delete` for archiving the current non-main session through the gateway session lifecycle.

## User Impact

- `/new Beispielsessionname`, `/new --name Beispielsessionname`, `/new name:Beispielsessionname`, and quoted `/new "Planning notes"` can start a fresh named session.
- Existing model forms such as `/new gpt-5.5`, `/new opus`, and native provider/model values keep falling through to model selection.
- `/close` and `/delete` archive the active non-main session from chat, but refuse main sessions and require `operator.admin` for internal gateway clients.

## Related

- Addresses part of #93422 (`/new <name>`; `/name` already exists from #88581)
- Addresses the chat-side individual session close/delete part of #90757
- Complements #90655 and #88479 for session renaming surfaces
- Needs to be considered alongside #95182 if canonical `title` replaces/augments `label`

## Evidence

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-delete-session.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-reset-hooks.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-name.test.ts` — 37 tests passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted with GitHub Copilot CLI. I reviewed the generated changes, resolved addressed Codex review conversations, and validated the touched command surfaces locally.

target.title: fix(subagents): preserve requester wake on visible delivery failure
target.body:
## Summary
- split requester wake success from visible message-tool delivery status for subagent completions
- preserve requester/parent wake when required group/channel visible delivery fails to produce message-tool evidence
- persist visible delivery failure details and mark linked detached task delivery as failed instead of delivered

## Why
For group/channel subagent completions, visible replies must still go through the message tool. When that visible delivery fails, the requester/parent wake should remain visible to orchestration instead of becoming a passive `pendingFinalDelivery` dead letter. The visible failure also must not be reported as successful task delivery.

This keeps the safety policy intact while preserving parent continuation and making task delivery accounting honest.

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior addressed:** subagent completion delivery where requester/parent handoff runs but the required group/channel visible reply does not produce message-tool evidence.

**Current PR head tested:** `09098d4ff67559d5ce492dd8c7e27d34b7dfceca`

**Real environment tested:** local OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch, using the patched subagent registry lifecycle and SQLite registry store with a redacted current-head proof harness. The harness used an active requester session shape, a group/channel route represented as `message_tool_only`, and injected the required-message-tool no-evidence delivery result at the lifecycle boundary so the patched persistence/task-delivery behavior could be observed without posting to a real workspace.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx '<redacted-temp-proof-script>'
```

**Evidence after fix:** terminal output from the current-head local OpenClaw lifecycle + SQLite restore proof harness:

## Tests
- `node --import tsx '<redacted-temp-proof-script>'` — current-head lifecycle + SQLite restore proof passed
- `corepack pnpm vitest run src/agents/subagent-registry.store.sqlite.test.ts src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts` — 4 files / 74 tests passed

## Summary

- Add regression coverage for empty patch, unrelated memory-core dry-run, malformed touched channel failure, and memory-core enable dry-run non-mutation.

## Safety boundary
This PR changes source/tests only. During validation I did not perform any OpenClaw runtime config write, Gateway restart, memory-core enablement, cleanup/reindex/recovery, recurring automation, or restricted-memory path change.
